title: Continuous glucose monitor review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting individual circumstances, preferences, caregiver role, device training, data sharing and clinician support needs.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact ADA device-principles section supports interval change, patient concerns, caregiver and training context, previous correspondence and clinician discussion without selecting a CGM device or changing treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting individual circumstances, preferences, caregiver role, device training, data sharing and clinician support needs.

source.title: 7. Diabetes Technology: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting CGM use, individual preference, continuity, periodic use, skin reaction, interfering-substance and reviewed-data context without changing therapy.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact CGM section supports associated symptoms, relevant negatives, focused assessment including skin reactions, home-monitoring review, clinician-entered plan, safety-net discussion and follow-up only when documented; no target or therapy change is generated.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting CGM use, individual preference, continuity, periodic use, skin reaction, interfering-substance and reviewed-data context without changing therapy.

source.title: 7. Diabetes Technology: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting monitoring method, circumstances, treatment context, suspected inaccuracy, interfering substances and reviewed blood-glucose records.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Blood-glucose monitoring backup and accuracy context were reviewed but no device or monitoring instruction is auto-generated.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting monitoring method, circumstances, treatment context, suspected inaccuracy, interfering substances and reviewed blood-glucose records.

source.title: 7. Diabetes Technology: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

sources_used.jurisdiction: International guideline published by a United States professional organisation

source_limitations: Device, wear time, data period, time in range, low or high patterns, alarms, accuracy, skin reaction, insulin regimen, interpretation and plan remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
